© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 3
™	STRAIGHT LINE PERSUASION
The straight-line syntax gives you the ultimate control.
You know where you are going before you get there, so you are able to move the client
where you want them to go.
FAST TRACK MODULE 4:
THE OUTER GAME OF SALES
Congratulations—you are about to complete the final module of the Fast Track Straight Line
Persuasion System! After the completion of this module, you will have learned the fundamentals and
game-changing secrets of the most powerful closers in business.
We last learned about the key principles of the Inner World and how you must first master this area
before you proceed to the next, the Outer World. The Outer World applies specific and practical skills
to begin seeing real results through the most successful closing patterns.
The essence of this system lies in the progression of certainty along the straight line of the sale.
You are applying the same principles in a specific order every time—where you are hitting certain
benchmarks as you progress along the line. This is the concept behind the words, “every sale is
the same.”
THE SUPER POWER OF PERSUASION
How do you take a good sale and turn it into a great sale? How do you take a good customer and
make them a great customer? What sets you apart from the rest?
The answer lies in your ability to:
Maintain ultra tight rapport
Resolve pain
Create absolute certainty
Overcome objections
Using the secrets below you will create a powerful arsenal of skills to perfect your abilities in these
four areas. Harness your super powers by becoming a master at each one!

-- 3 of 8 --

JORDAN BELFORT
© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 4
TIP
Create your script from the very best versions—or pieces—from different presentations to
create one cohesive presentation.
SECRETS TO THE PERFECT SALE
The Art of Straight Line Script Writing
Learn to write and use a script without it sounding like you are using one. A script provides you with
certain language patterns and information that is already memorized in your head. It is a compilation
of the best version of information you discuss with the prospect.
The Art of Looping
Looping can be a useful tool to use when wanting to increase certainty. Certainty will naturally wane
as time goes by. Remember the three 10s from the second module, by using the art of looping you
can loop back in information to raise the prospect’s certainty level at the critical time. Even if you are
using an airtight script, the prospect will generally give you reasons why they cannot say “Yes” to
the sale. Learning to take this so called “stall” and transforming it into certainty will ensure you get a
prospect to ultimately say, “Yes.”
10 	10	10
ELEMENT #1
FIRST 10
They love
your product
ELEMENT #2
SECOND 10
They trust and
connect with you.
ELEMENT #3
THIRD 10
They trust and
connect with
your company.
YOU MAKE 3 LOOPS TO CAPTURE THE THREE 10S

-- 4 of 8 --

© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 5
™	STRAIGHT LINE PERSUASION
Handling Objections
The process of the close begins when you first hear a “stall” from the prospect (i.e. “I need to ask
my spouse,” or, “I have to think about it.”). In order to handle whatever objection you are faced with,
transition to permission for a question (i.e. “I hear what you’re saying, but let me ask you a question.”).
The Meaning of No
In the diagram to the right, as you get closer to the water line—or closing of the sale, you are making
massive progress with
certainty. Eventually, the
prospect will jump above the
water line and take action.
When your clients says, “No,”
it does not mean they will not
buy, it simply means they do
not have absolute certainty.
Retrain your brain to think of
the word, “No” as a sign that
progress is being made.
TIPS
• 	Script out all your questions.
• 	Start out with big picture questions.
• 	Move from more general to specific questions.
• 	Your questions should be uncovering their reasons “why.”
The Syntax of Logic and Emotion
First make a logical case in your presentation—the logical brain acts as the detector for information
you either agree or disagree with. Once you satisfy the logical brain you free up the emotional side,
which is the reason they ultimately purchase.
Asking Questions
Questions are essential throughout a presentation to gather valuable intelligence about your prospect.
Remember to ask for permission to ask questions. This acts as a perfect transition to gain rapport and
progress them along the straight line.
TIP
Lowering the action threshold will lower the water line.
“YES”
“No”

-- 5 of 8 --

JORDAN BELFORT
© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 6
The Ultimate Equation of Closing a Sale
ENERGY IN 	BENEFITS OUT=
During your close, if someone has to expend a significant amount of energy to receive little benefits,
it is not likely they will take action. If they expend very little energy, and receive a massive amount of
benefit, they will tend to take action. The transaction must be easy for the prospect. Taking the first
step in the process must be very simple.
The True Meaning of Straight Line Persuasion
The Straight Line System is about your ability to not only influence others, but yourself. It is about
uncovering limiting beliefs and replacing them with new empowering ones.
Learn from Jordan’s mistakes—“greed is not good, it is ambition that is good.” When you
develop the Straight Line System’s ultimate power, only use it to help people get the things
they need and do the things they should do.

-- 6 of 8 --

© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 7
™	STRAIGHT LINE PERSUASION
ACTION PLAN
STEP 1: SCRIPTING A PERFECT CLOSE
Let’s use what you learned about the art of script writing to develop your perfect script. We’ll do
the close together, but then use this same method to develop the transitions, body, and opening.
Remember, you are primary building rapport through tonality and body language during the first four
seconds, so pay special attention to this when scripting your open.
Brainstorm. Think of some of your most powerful sales interactions. What were some of the closing
language patterns you used? Write down a script of how you believed you closed that sale.
Record. Record the close portion of the next ten sales conversations you have.
Disclaimer: Do not record the prospect without permission, as it is illegal unless they know they are
being recorded.
Rank. On a scale of 1-10, with 1 being the worst close to 10 being the perfect close, rate each of your
sales conversations. Model only the conversations where you closed the sale, but also learn from
your mistakes.
Review. Now, review your top three rated closes. What made them so successful? Were you able to
establish the fundamentals learned from this fast track system? What skills did you use? Write down
the key phrases and language patterns to script out your perfect close.

-- 7 of 8 --

JORDAN BELFORT
© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 8
Find an Expert. Do you have a colleague or friend in your field who you would consider highly
successful? If so, ask them permission to listen to the close portion of their conversations with prospects.
What skills, phrases, and language patterns are they using to get the prospect to take action?
STEP 2: YOUR HIGHER PURPOSE
Whether it is your own personal limitations or those of your prospect, the Straight Line System is all
about freeing people from the beliefs that hold them back and transforming them into empowering ones.
Think about the product or service that you are selling. If you were completely honest, what would
some of your limiting beliefs be about what you are selling to people? If you cannot think of any, think
about the sales process in general.
Now, turn that limiting belief into something more empowering. What do you really believe is true
about this product or service? How can it empower or help people? What does it do for their family?
How can it change lives? What does it help you to do and create?
Tip: If you ever find yourself not selling at the highest level, concentrate on the higher purpose of what
you are selling—or the more empowering beliefs—for a few minutes. You will transform the way you
talk to your clients by doing this at the beginning of everyday.

-- 8 of 8 --
